content.text: *"If the root is holy, so are the branches."*{Romans 11:16}{Rooted trees give hierarchy to what would otherwise be a flat structure.}{Arthur Cayley}

A **rooted tree** has one vertex designated as the 
root, giving the tree a natural hierarchy.

content.title: Rooted Tree Terminology
content.content:
In a rooted tree with root $r$:

- The **parent** of $v \neq r$ is the next vertex toward $r$.

- The **children** of $v$ are vertices for which $v$ is parent.

- **Siblings** share the same parent.

- **Ancestors** of $v$: vertices on path from $v$ to $r$.

- **Descendants** of $v$: vertices having $v$ as ancestor.

- A **leaf** has no children; an **internal vertex** has 
 children.

- The **subtree** rooted at $v$ contains $v$ and all descendants.

content.title: Level and Height
content.content:
The **level** of vertex $v$ is the length of the path from $r$ to $v$.

The **height** of a tree is the maximum level of any vertex.

content.title: Binary Tree
content.content:
A **binary tree** is a rooted tree where each 
vertex has at most 2 children, designated as **left child** and 
**right child**.

A **full binary tree** has every internal 
vertex with exactly 2 children.

A **complete binary tree** is full with all leaves at the same level.

content.title: Binary Tree Properties
content.content:
A binary tree of height $h$ has:

- At most $2^h$ leaves

- At most $2^{h+1} - 1$ vertices

A full binary tree with $n$ internal vertices has $n + 1$ leaves.
content.label: Theorem

content.title: Full binary tree
content.problem:
A full binary tree of height 3 has at most $2^3 = 8$ leaves and 
$2^4 - 1 = 15$ total vertices.

content.text: Three standard ways to visit all nodes of a binary tree:

content.title: Tree Traversal Orders
content.content:
- **Preorder**: Visit root, then left subtree, then right subtree.

- **Inorder**: Visit left subtree, then root, then right subtree.

- **Postorder**: Visit left subtree, then right subtree, then root.

content.title: Expression tree

marginNotes.text:
A shoot will come up from the stump of Jesse; 
from his roots a Branch will bear fruit.
